channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Sam Altman] LIVE: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Speaks at BlackRock Infrastructure Summit | DRM News | AI1F
item.author: DRM News
item.description:
DRM News · 39 min
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman spoke at the BlackRock U.S. Infrastructure Summit in Washington, D.C. He discussed AI's rapid adoption in business, the scaling of AI infrastructure, the pursuit of artificial general intelligence, and OpenAI's $110 billion funding round. Leaders from government, technology, and finance gathered to discuss investment, infrastructure development, and the role of emerging technologies in economic growth.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "LLMs Are A Dead End": An Exclusive Interview With The Genius Father of AI | Yann LeCun
item.author: This Is The World
item.description:
This Is The World · 51 min
An exclusive interview with Yann LeCun, Turing Award-winning AI researcher and Chief AI Scientist at Meta, hosted by Matt Kawecki on This Is The World podcast. LeCun discusses why he believes large language models represent a dead end for achieving human-level AI, and outlines his vision for an alternative path — centered on world models and self-supervised learning — toward more general artificial intelligence.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "Perspectives on IA" : conf. de Yann LeCun, WinterWeek – Graduate School – Univ. Gustave Eiffel
item.author: Université Gustave Eiffel
item.description:
Université Gustave Eiffel · 126 min
Yann LeCun, 2018 Turing Award laureate and founder of AMI Labs, delivered a lecture titled 「Perspectives on AI」 at the WinterWeek for Research Training hosted by the Graduate School of Université Gustave Eiffel. The talk, originally given on February 11, 2026, covers his current perspectives on the state and future of artificial intelligence, presented in French.

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The AI Scenario Nobody Wants To Talk About | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min
A clip from The Diary of a CEO featuring Geoffrey Hinton, known as the 「Godfather of AI.」 After more than 50 years helping build the neural network foundations of today's AI systems, Hinton explains why he now sounds the alarm about the technology's risks — including autonomous weapons, AI-driven cyber attacks, and other scenarios he believes humanity must urgently address.

item.title: [Andrew Ng] EducAIted Voices: Dr. Andrew Ng, Exploring Computer Science & K12 Education
item.author: EducAIted
item.description:
EducAIted · 28 min
The premiere episode of EducAIted Voices features Dr. Andrew Ng — founder of DeepLearning.AI and Coursera co-founder — in conversation with hosts Emma Ludwick and Dr. Bailey Ubellacker. The discussion focuses on AI in K-12 education: how computer science education is evolving, the opportunities AI presents for teachers and students, and how educators can navigate this rapidly changing landscape.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang delivered his annual keynote at GTC 2026 in San Jose, California. The speech covered the latest GPU and AI computing announcements, NVIDIA's acqui-hire of AI inference chip designer Groq, partnerships with Nebius and other AI cloud providers, and Huang's broader vision for the future of AI infrastructure and physical AI.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang and the Reinvention of Computing
item.author: APPLIED ENGLISH LABS
item.description:
APPLIED ENGLISH LABS · 51 min
In this interview, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang traces the evolution of accelerated computing — from early GPU innovations in video games to the deep learning breakthroughs they enabled. He discusses NVIDIA's strategic bets over the decades, the rise of physical AI using digital twins and world models, and how the company positioned itself at the center of the AI revolution.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] 黃仁勳最新訪談：AI 自駕已不是科幻，真正革命才剛開始｜Jensen Huang’s Interview: AI Self-Driving Is No Longer Science Fiction
item.author: New SciTech 新科技
item.description:
New SciTech 新科技 · 22 min
Jensen Huang discusses AI-powered autonomous driving, end-to-end models, safety stacks, and the future of robotaxis, framing autonomous driving as the preview of a coming transportation revolution rather than just a technology demo.

item.title: [Channel: Dwarkesh Patel] Dylan Patel — The single biggest bottleneck to scaling AI compute
item.author: Dwarkesh Patel
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel · 151 min
Dylan Patel, founder of SemiAnalysis, joins Dwarkesh Patel for a deep dive into the three major bottlenecks to scaling AI compute: logic (chips), memory, and power. The conversation covers the economics of AI labs, hyperscalers, foundries, and fab equipment manufacturers, including TSMC allocation dynamics, ASML as a long-term constraint by 2030, the coming memory crunch, power scaling in the U.S., and the geopolitics of Taiwan and China in the semiconductor race.
Timestamps: 00:00 – Why an H100 is worth more today than 3 years ago 00:24 – Nvidia secured TSMC allocation early; Google is getting squeezed 00:34 – ASML will be the #1 constraint for AI compute scaling by 2030 00:55 – Can't we just use TSMC's older fabs? 01:05 – When will China outscale the West in semis? 01:16 – The enormous incoming memory crunch 01:42 – Scaling power in the US will not be a problem 02:14 – Why aren't more hedge funds making the AGI trade? 02:18 – Will TSMC kick Apple out from N2? 02:24 – Robots and Taiwan risk

item.title: [Channel: Machine Learning Street Talk] When AI Discovers the Next Transformer — Robert Lange
item.author: Machine Learning Street Talk
item.description:
Machine Learning Street Talk · 78 min
Robert Lange, founding researcher at Sakana AI, joins Tim Scarfe on Machine Learning Street Talk to discuss Shinka Evolve — a framework combining LLMs with evolutionary algorithms for open-ended program search. The conversation explores why systems like AlphaEvolve get stuck (they need humans to define the right problem), how Shinka tries to co-evolve problems and solutions automatically using MAP-Elites and UCB bandits, and concrete results including state-of-the-art circle packing and second place in AtCoder competitive programming. They also debate whether automated research pipelines can produce real science, and where AI-assisted scientific discovery is headed in the next 5–20 years.
Timestamps: 00:00 – Introduction 00:04 – AlphaEvolve's blind spot: co-evolving problems with solutions 00:09 – Unknown unknowns, POET, and auto-curricula for AI science 00:14 – MAP-Elites and quality-diversity: Shinka's evolutionary architecture 00:28 – UCB bandits, mutations, and the vibe research vision 00:40 – Scaling Shinka: meta-evolution and the three-axis model 00:47 – Applications, ARC-AGI and the future of work 00:57 – The AI Scientist and the human co-pilot 01:06 – AI Scientist v2, slop critique and future of scientific publishing

item.title: [Channel: Y Combinator] India’s Fastest Growing AI Startup
item.author: Y Combinator
item.description:
Y Combinator · 40 min
Y Combinator's Lightcone podcast features Mukund and Madhav Jha, co-founders of Emergent — an AI platform that lets anyone build and ship production-ready software without coding. In eight months, users created over 7 million apps on the platform, with the number doubling in the last 45 days. The conversation covers how they built one of the most capable AI coding agents, why they focused on non-technical users, and what it's like building a global AI product from India.
Chapters: 00:00 – What Is Emergent? 01:18 – Founder Backstory 02:09 – From AI Testing to General Coding Agents 04:18 – The Pivot to Non-Technical Users 05:22 – Why Second Movers Can Win in AI 09:04 – Building for Production, Not Just Prototypes 18:21 – Live Demo: Building Apps with Emergent 24:40 – How Emergent Hires and Runs a Lean Team 29:04 – Is SaaS Dead? The Rise of Personalized Software 34:04 – The Future: Niche Apps, Solo Builders and AI Agency
